name: Sapphire Gallery
description: Historic sapphire mining area near Helena with stunning blue sapphires. Open to public rockhounders during designated seasons. Professional-grade specimens and collector-quality gems available.

name: Gem Valley Mine
description: Premier garnet mining location in central Idaho's gem valley. Fee-dig operation with excellent finds of deep red garnets. Equipment rental available.

name: Sunstone Point
description: Famous sunstone collecting area in south-central Oregon. Beautiful peachy-orange sunstones are abundant. Permits required but easily obtained from local BLM office.

name: Jade Cove
description: Coastal jade collecting site on California's Big Sur. World-class nephrite jade specimens found along beaches. Tide-dependent access.

name: Sleeping Beauty Mine
description: Iconic turquoise mining area producing the famous Sleeping Beauty turquoise. Tour and collect on designated property. Educational tours available.

name: Agate Basin
description: Large agate fields with colorful banded specimens. Public lands with abundant surface deposits. Great for beginners and families.

name: Virgin Valley Opal
description: World-famous opal deposits in northern Nevada. Private claims available to collect. Beautiful fire opals and common opals found.

name: Petrified Forest Park
description: Petrified wood collecting area in East Texas. Colorful specimens of various sizes. Public park with designated rockhounding areas.

name: Petoskey Stone Beach
description: Fossil coral deposits on Lake Michigan beaches. Petoskey stones are Michigan's state stone. Easy access and excellent specimens.

name: Hiddenite Emerald Mine
description: Historic emerald mining area in western North Carolina. Fee-dig mine with professional equipment available. Beautiful green specimens.
